title: erythrophleum densiflorum
intro: Erythrophleum densiflorum, also known as the subabwa tree, is a tropical tree species native to the savannas and grasslands of sub-Saharan Africa. The bark of this tree has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Erythrophleum densiflorum include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a natural remedy for wounds and snake bites. Erythrophleum densiflorum can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
